summary: Ukrainian drones are increasingly striking rear areas at distances of 40+ km, with loitering munitions now targeting equipment up to 100 km from the front line, combining cheap mass-produced strike drones with expensive foreign analogues. The enemy has regained leadership in the "small sky" through a 2.5-fold increase in drone numbers and improved range and payload capacity, including new portable drones like the XDOWN STUD, which can fly at 300 km/h with a 64 km range. On the ground, FPV drones continue to destroy Russian forces, including soldiers using motorcycles and those attempting to surrender, while Russian forces are reducing FPV control frequency and increasing antenna size on electronic warfare systems.
